# PaperFigureSkill

Claude Code skill for publication-quality figures, plots, and diagrams. Render-view-fix iteration loop catches visual defects at print size before submission.

![Torus example](docs/torus.png)

*Hero image generated by `skills/PaperFigureSkill/examples/torus.py` — single-column square, viridis surface shaded by depth, Helvetica embedded as TrueType in the PDF. Example outputs under `skills/PaperFigureSkill/figures/` are gitignored; only this README copy lives in `docs/`.*

## Install

Add the marketplace in Claude Code:

```
/plugin marketplace add chrischoy/PaperFigureSkill
```

Install the plugin:

```
/plugin install PaperFigureSkill@PaperFigureSkill
```

Reload plugins:

```
/reload-plugins
```

The skill auto-triggers on phrases like "figure for my paper", "plot for the manuscript", "architecture diagram", "publication-quality", "submission-quality figure", or any reference to LaTeX, NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, IEEE, ACM, Nature, Science, or arXiv.

If you prefer to install by hand, clone the repo into your skills directory:

```
git clone https://github.com/chrischoy/PaperFigureSkill ~/.claude/skills/PaperFigureSkill-src
ln -s ~/.claude/skills/PaperFigureSkill-src/skills/PaperFigureSkill ~/.claude/skills/PaperFigureSkill
/reload-skills
```

After install, install the Python deps in your host environment (Claude Code does not manage Python). Clone the repo and install from there — the plugin install path is implementation-defined and not a stable place to invoke pip from:

```
git clone https://github.com/chrischoy/PaperFigureSkill /tmp/PaperFigureSkill
pip install -r /tmp/PaperFigureSkill/requirements.txt
pip install -r /tmp/PaperFigureSkill/requirements-extras.txt   # optional
```

For graphviz diagrams, also install the `dot` binary (`brew install graphviz` / `apt install graphviz`).

One simple first run, inside Claude Code:

```
Make a publication-quality 3D plot of a torus
```

Claude renders `figures/fig_torus.{pdf,svg,png}` in your working directory and views the PNG to verify legibility at print size. The matching script also lives at `skills/PaperFigureSkill/examples/torus.py` for direct execution.

## Layout

```
.claude-plugin/
  marketplace.json         — declares this repo as a single-plugin marketplace
  plugin.json              — plugin metadata
skills/
  PaperFigureSkill/
    SKILL.md               — entry point; loaded into Claude's context
    scripts/
      pubstyle.py          — publication rcParams (fonts, spines, vector-safe)
      colors.py            — colorblind-safe palettes (Okabe-Ito, Tol Muted)
      export.py            — atomic multi-format save (PDF + SVG + PNG)
    references/
      plots.md             — line, bar, scatter, heatmap, violin, multi-panel,
                             histogram, ablation, 3D surface
      diagrams.md          — matplotlib / graphviz / hand-SVG patterns
      iteration.md         — render→view→fix loop + defect catalog
      checklist.md         — pre-submission QA
    examples/
      torus.py             — runnable end-to-end 3D surface example
    figures/               — example outputs
README.md
requirements.txt           — core Python deps (host env, not Claude Code)
requirements-extras.txt    — optional deps for diagram/SVG/scipy patterns
```

## Quick start (without Claude Code)

```python
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "<path-to-repo>/skills/PaperFigureSkill/scripts")

import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pubstyle, colors, export

pubstyle.apply()
colors.apply_cycle()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=pubstyle.figsize("single"))
ax.plot([1, 2, 3], [1, 4, 2])
ax.set_xlabel("x"); ax.set_ylabel("y")

export.save(fig, "fig_demo")    # writes ./figures/fig_demo.{pdf,svg,png}
```

For venue-specific defaults: `pubstyle.apply(venue="ieee")` (also `neurips`, `icml`, `iclr`, `acm`, `nature`).

## What it fixes

Defaults matplotlib ships with are designed for screen exploration, not print. This skill fixes the most common "default matplotlib" tells:

- Embedded TrueType fonts in PDFs (`pdf.fonttype = 42`) — journals reject Type 3.
- Helvetica/Arial body text + matching `stixsans` math glyphs.
- Top/right spines off, subtle ticks, no gridlines by default.
- Colorblind-safe categorical palette (Okabe-Ito) + perceptually uniform sequential (`viridis`/`cividis`) and diverging (`RdBu_r`) recommendations.
- Print-size figure dimensions baked into the helpers, so 8 pt fonts stay 8 pt.
- `export.save()` writes PDF + SVG + PNG together with `bbox_inches="tight"`, atomically (temp + replace), with path-traversal guard on the name.

## Iteration loop

Render → view PNG at native resolution → list defects → apply fixes from `references/iteration.md` catalog → re-render. Cap at two cycles. The PNG renders at 300 DPI from a figure whose dimensions are the actual print size, so on-screen pixels match what readers see on paper.
